body:
Dear Diane,

I write to follow up on our earlier conversation regarding my morning routine, specifically the matter of the tea preparation. As I mentioned, the ritual begins shortly after I rise  -  generally by six-fifteen  -  and I find that the discipline of it sets a reliable tone for the remainder of the day.

This morning I used the Yixing pot Linda purchased in 2019 for the first time in several weeks. The difference in the resulting cup was, I must confess, not inconsiderable. I have since concluded that the clay does something useful to the mineral character of the Silver Spring tap water, though I hesitate to claim a scientific basis for that assessment.

If it would assist you in updating the file, the tea in current rotation is a Yunnan Dian Hong  -  a black variety, taken without milk or sugar. The temperature I target is approximately ninety-five degrees Celsius, steeped for three minutes precisely.

I trust this level of detail is what was requested. Please let me know if any clarification is required before our next check-in.

Respectfully,
Tony Nguyen Sr.

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

Thank you for the thorough follow-up  -  this is exactly the detail that helps us keep your profile current and accurate.

I have updated the record to reflect the Yixing pot and the Yunnan Dian Hong black tea, noting the preparation specifics: ninety-five degrees Celsius, three-minute steep, no additions. I also logged the approximate start time of six-fifteen, which aligns well with the overall morning schedule we have on file.

If anything changes in the rotation  -  a seasonal tea, a different vessel  -  please do not hesitate to send a brief note and I will amend the record accordingly. We want the file to reflect your actual routine, not an approximation of it.

I will schedule our next check-in for the same time as before. In the meantime, I hope the Yixing pot continues to cooperate.

Warm regards,
Diane Nguyen
Client Support Coordinator

Dear Diane,

I am writing to follow up on our conversation of last Thursday regarding the morning medication protocol. As you will recall, I raised a concern about the sequencing of the Accu-Chek Guide reading relative to the Metformin 1000mg dose  -  specifically, whether the current order aligns with the guidance Dr. Okafor's office provided in March.

I completed this morning's routine as previously discussed, but I would appreciate written confirmation of the correct sequence before I adjust anything further. Consistency matters to me, and I prefer to have the procedure documented rather than rely on my memory alone.

Please advise at your earliest convenience. I am available by telephone each weekday between ten in the morning and noon, or by reply to this message.

Respectfully,
Tony M. Nguyen Sr.

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

Thank you for writing, and I apologize that this confirmation did not reach you sooner.

Having reviewed the notes from your March appointment, the correct sequence is as follows: complete the Accu-Chek Guide reading first, record the result in your log, and then take the morning Metformin 1000mg, Lisinopril 20mg, and Hydrochlorothiazide 12.5mg together with a full glass of water. Dr. Okafor's office confirmed that the reading should precede the medications, not follow them.

I have attached a one-page summary you may keep beside your morning tea station for easy reference. If anything in that document conflicts with what you have been doing, please do call the office before making any change.

Warm regards,
Diane Nguyen
Patient Support Services

Dear Diane,

The attached summary arrived clearly and has been printed and placed accordingly  -  it is now beside the kettle, which is precisely where it will be consulted each morning before I proceed.

I appreciate the prompt clarification. I had been performing the steps in the reverse order for approximately two weeks, which is long enough to warrant a note to Dr. Okafor's office. I will telephone them tomorrow morning, after my walk on Sligo Creek Trail, to ensure they are aware of the discrepancy and have documented it in the file.

This matter is now resolved on my end. Should anything further be required of me, you have my contact information.

With appreciation,
Tony M. Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Diane,

I am writing to follow up on our exchange of last Tuesday regarding the study module files to be made available through the federation's digital lending portal. You had indicated that the annotated endgame collections  -  rook endings and minor piece technique  -  would be accessible by end of the week.

As of this morning's study session, the portal continues to display only the opening theory materials, specifically the Nimzo-Indian and King's Indian repertoire files. The endgame section remains inaccessible. I rely on the morning window to work through these positions methodically, and the interruption has proved more disruptive than one might anticipate for what is, after all, a cataloguing matter.

If there is a technical delay or a revised timeline, I would appreciate notification so that I may plan accordingly. I am also willing to receive the files directly by electronic mail if that proves more expedient than waiting on the portal.

Respectfully,
Tony Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

Thank you for the follow-up. I have confirmed with our technical team that the endgame files were uploaded Thursday evening; however, a permissions configuration error is preventing member access to that category. The issue has been escalated and is expected to be resolved within twenty-four hours.

In the meantime, I have attached the three core endgame files directly to this message so that your morning sessions need not be further interrupted. They are organized by classification: rook-and-pawn endings, king and pawn structures, and minor piece technique.

Please let me know if the attachments do not come through or if you require additional materials. I will send a follow-up confirmation once portal access has been fully restored.

Warm regards,
Diane Nguyen
Member Support, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Diane,

The attachments arrived without difficulty, and I worked through the first section on rook-and-pawn endings this morning  -  a subject I find, I will confess, considerably more tractable on paper than on screen. The material is precisely what I had in mind.

I have one additional request. The federation's opening theory archive lists a file titled "Petroff Defense  -  Structural Themes," which I have been unable to locate through the search function. It does not appear among the Nimzo-Indian or King's Indian materials previously accessible to me. If the file exists in the system, I would be grateful for a direct link or an attached copy at your convenience.

I appreciate the prompt handling of the previous matter.

Regards,
Tony Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

I am glad the endgame materials proved useful. I have located the Petroff Defense file  -  it was catalogued under "Classical Defenses" rather than the opening-specific folders, which explains why the search function did not surface it. I have attached it to this message.

I have also flagged the cataloguing inconsistency to the content team so that the file will be properly indexed going forward. Portal access to all endgame materials has been restored as of this morning, so the full library should now be available to you directly without further workaround.

Please do not hesitate to reach out if anything else is needed. It is always a pleasure to assist.

Best regards,
Diane Nguyen
Member Support, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Diane,

I completed this afternoon's circuit on Sligo Creek Trail without difficulty. The weather was cooperative  -  an overcast sky kept the temperature reasonable, and the trail was populated but not congested. I managed the full three miles, outbound and return, in just under one hour, which I consider a satisfactory pace given the humidity.

I did wish to note one matter for the record: the footbridge near the Wayne Avenue crossing has a plank that appears to have come partially loose. I navigated around it without incident, but it is the sort of defect that becomes a genuine hazard once summer trail use peaks. I thought it worth flagging in the event anyone has a contact at Montgomery County Parks.

Linda joins me in sending regards. I anticipate no schedule conflicts for our call tomorrow.

Respectfully,
Tony Nguyen

Mr. Nguyen,

Thank you for the thorough report  -  I am glad to hear the walk was completed without incident and that the conditions were manageable. Three miles in just under an hour is a commendable pace.

I have made a note regarding the loose planking near Wayne Avenue and will reach out to our contact at Montgomery County Parks to request an inspection. You are right that early intervention is far preferable to waiting until a complaint is filed after the fact.

Our call is confirmed for tomorrow. Should anything arise beforehand, please do not hesitate to reach me directly.

Warm regards,
Diane Nguyen

Dear Ms. Nguyen,

I am writing to follow up on ticket reference #GMT-88241, submitted on June 14th regarding the Gmail API integration we use for the Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation's organizational account. I received an automated acknowledgment at the time of submission but have not yet received a substantive update.

The matter is as follows: the API credentials powering our automated tournament notification system ceased functioning on or around June 14th. Outbound messages to our member roster  -  approximately 340 registered players  -  have since been queued without delivery. The communications pertaining to our spring classical tournament, which were due to reach members earlier this week, remain unsent.

I would be grateful for confirmation that the ticket has been assigned to a qualified resource, and for an estimated timeline for resolution. If account verification is required on my end, I am prepared to furnish whatever documentation is necessary.

Respectfully,
Tony M. Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

Thank you for following up, and I apologize for the delay in substantive communication. I can confirm your ticket has been reviewed and escalated to our API support team.

Preliminary investigation suggests the OAuth 2.0 token associated with your service account expired without automatic renewal  -  typically the result of a security setting change or a quota anomaly. I have flagged the account for priority review.

Could you please confirm whether any changes were made to your Google Workspace administrator settings between June 10th and June 14th? Additionally, if you have access to the Google Cloud Console, I would ask that you verify whether the Gmail API remains enabled under APIs and Services for your project.

We expect to have a full diagnostic ready within 24 business hours. I appreciate your patience and regret any disruption to your federation's operations.

Sincerely,
Diane Nguyen
Technical Support Specialist

Dear Ms. Nguyen,

Thank you for the expeditious escalation and for the clarity of your preliminary assessment. To address your questions directly: no administrative changes were made to the Workspace settings during the period you specified. I have verified this with the individual who assists with the technical administration of the account.

Regarding the Cloud Console: I have located the APIs and Services dashboard and confirmed that the Gmail API is listed as enabled. I note, however, that one of the OAuth client credential entries displays a warning icon that was absent during my last review approximately three weeks prior. I have captured a screenshot and can provide it upon request.

I will be available by electronic mail throughout the day, with the exception of my afternoon hours between approximately 2:00 and 4:00 p.m. The federation's tournament season is presently active, and member communication is of some operational consequence, so I would welcome whatever pace the matter permits.

Respectfully,
Tony M. Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Diane,

I am writing to follow up on the matter we discussed last Thursday regarding the Google Calendar API integration for the Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation's event scheduling system. As I noted at the time, the API is failing to push our tournament calendar entries  -  specifically the June and July round-robin schedules  -  to member accounts with external Google Workspace domains.

I have since confirmed that the service account credentials were rotated on June 9th by our webmaster, and I believe this may be the root cause. The federation's board meeting is scheduled for June 24th, and I would very much prefer to have the calendar synchronization restored prior to that date so that directors receive their automated reminders.

Could you confirm whether the OAuth scope configuration requires updating following a credential rotation, and if so, provide the precise steps required?

Respectfully,
Tony Michael Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

Thank you for the detailed follow-up. You are correct that rotating the service account credentials requires re-authorization of the OAuth scopes  -  the existing token is invalidated upon key rotation, and the Calendar API will silently fail on push operations until new consent is granted.

To resolve this, please have your webmaster navigate to the Google Cloud Console, select the relevant project, and under "APIs & Services > Credentials," generate a new OAuth 2.0 client secret. The service account will then need to be re-granted domain-wide delegation with the scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar. I am attaching a step-by-step guide for your reference.

Please do not hesitate to reach out if you encounter any difficulty before your June 24th board meeting. We want to ensure your directors receive their reminders without interruption.

Warm regards,
Diane Nguyen
Technical Support Specialist

Dear Diane,

Thank you for the precise explanation. I forwarded your instructions to our webmaster, Marcus, yesterday afternoon, and he completed the re-authorization this morning. I can confirm that a test event  -  a preliminary entry for our Silver Spring club's July 12th endgame workshop  -  was successfully pushed to three member accounts, including one on an external Workspace domain.

I do have one remaining concern. The API appears to be sending duplicate notifications for events that were originally created prior to the credential rotation. Several members have written to me this afternoon noting that they received two calendar invitations for the same July session. I would appreciate guidance on whether there is a straightforward method to suppress or retract the redundant entries without disrupting the corrected records.

Again, I am grateful for your assistance on this matter.

Respectfully,
Tony Michael Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

I am pleased to hear the re-authorization was completed successfully  -  and a July endgame workshop sounds like a fine test case.

Regarding the duplicate invitations: this is a known behavior when a service account re-syncs previously created events after a credential gap. The cleanest resolution is to use the Calendar API's events.delete endpoint to remove the orphaned duplicate entries by their event ID, then issue a single events.patch call to re-confirm the canonical record. Alternatively, if the duplicates were created under the old credential scope, your webmaster can identify them in the Google Calendar UI by their creation timestamp and delete them manually without touching the API.

I have escalated a note to our engineering team to document this edge case more prominently in our rotation guide, so future administrators are forewarned. If you continue to see anomalies after removing the duplicates, please reply to this thread and I will escalate to tier-two support.

Best regards,
Diane Nguyen
Technical Support Specialist

Dear Ms. Nguyen,

I am writing to follow up on ticket #DR-4471, submitted last Thursday, regarding the Google Drive API integration for the Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation's document repository. As of this morning, the issue persists: service account credentials authenticated without error, yet API calls to the shared tournament archive folder continue to return a 403 Forbidden response.

I attempted the remediation you outlined  -  revoking and regenerating the service account key, then re-granting Editor access at the folder level  -  during my study session this morning. The behavior is unchanged. The Federation's secretary has been unable to upload the May rapid-play scoresheets, and the May board minutes remain inaccessible to the four officers who require them.

Please advise whether escalation to your Workspace integration team is warranted, or whether there is an additional configuration step I may have overlooked. I am available by reply throughout the day.

Respectfully,
Tony M. Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation
Silver Spring, MD

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

Thank you for the detailed follow-up on ticket #DR-4471. I reviewed the account logs following your message and identified the root cause: the service account was granted folder-level Editor access, but the shared drive itself has the setting "Restrict sharing to users in [your domain]" enabled, which overrides folder-level permissions for external service accounts.

To resolve this, a Workspace administrator will need to navigate to the shared drive settings and either exempt the service account from the domain restriction or enable the "Allow access to this shared drive for external users" option. I have drafted the exact steps and attached them to the ticket. If you can forward the ticket reference to whoever holds admin rights for your Federation's Workspace account, they should be able to complete the change in under five minutes.

Please do not hesitate to reply if any step is unclear or if the issue persists after the configuration is updated. I will hold the ticket open through end of week.

Warm regards,
Diane Nguyen
Workspace Support Specialist
Google Customer Support

Dear Ms. Nguyen,

I write to follow up on our exchange of last Thursday regarding the Obsidian API integration I have been configuring for the Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation's research vault. You indicated at that time that the authentication token conflict would be resolved within 48 hours; I have not, however, received any confirmation that the matter has been addressed.

For context: I rely on this integration to maintain structured notes on opening theory and endgame positions  -  material I review during my morning study sessions before the day's other obligations begin. A disruption to that workflow is manageable in the short term, but the Federation's spring tournament cycle begins in three weeks and preparation records must be accessible.

I would be grateful if you could confirm the current ticket status and advise whether any action is required on my end.

Respectfully,
Tony M. Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation

Dear Mr. Nguyen,

Thank you for following up, and I apologize unreservedly for the absence of communication. The ticket was escalated to our backend team and I should have provided an interim status update rather than leaving you without a response. That was an oversight I will not repeat.

The root cause has been identified: your API token was provisioned under a legacy authentication endpoint deprecated in release 0.3.4. I have issued a replacement token under the current scheme and delivered it to the email address on file. To restore the connection, please navigate to Settings > Community Plugins > Obsidian Local REST API in your vault and replace the existing token value with the one provided.

I will hold this ticket open until you confirm resolution. Please do not hesitate to write back if any step is unclear or if the issue persists.

Warm regards,
Diane Nguyen
Customer Support, Obsidian API Team

Dear Ms. Nguyen,

I am pleased to report that the replacement token functioned exactly as described. The vault connected on the first attempt following the update, and both retrieval and write operations are now performing without error.

I verified the configuration this morning before my chess study session and confirmed that the Federation's tournament preparation files are fully accessible. The disruption to my workflow is resolved.

I appreciate the precision of your remediation instructions; they spared me a considerable amount of diagnostic effort. I now understand the version dependency and will check the endpoint schema first if a similar issue arises in future. The ticket may be closed at your discretion.

Respectfully,
Tony M. Nguyen Sr.
President, Mid-Atlantic Community Chess Federation
